issues.title: Errors: map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses

tests.scope: Confirm that a real 404 from Alma SANDBOX (GET on a synthetic invalid user_primary_id) is dispatched through _classify_error and raises the typed AlmaResourceNotFoundError subclass — not the bare AlmaAPIError. Exercises the HTTP-status fallback path (#9 AC: status-fallback wiring) end-to-end against a live error response.

needsHumanInput.description: A synthetic user_primary_id known NOT to exist in SANDBOX. Used to deliberately trigger a 404 so the typed-error dispatch can be observed against a live Alma response. Do not use a real user.

unmappable.ac: Public API exports the new classes from `almaapitk.__init__` and the lazy-import map in `_lazy_imports`.
unmappable.reason: Pure-Python import-surface invariant — covered by tests/test_public_api_contract.py and the unit tests; no Alma API surface to exercise.
Test-file content invariant — verified by the test suite itself; no SANDBOX call would observe it.

unmappable.ac: Registry lives at module level so it can be extended with one-line additions as we discover more codes.
unmappable.reason: Module-structure invariant — covered by the ERROR_CODE_REGISTRY assertions in tests/unit/client/test_error_classification.py; not observable via SANDBOX.

unmappable.ac: Codes are documented in a single table, cross-linked with the `alma-api-expert` skill.
unmappable.reason: Documentation deliverable — verified by docs review, not by an API call.

issues.title: Errors: propagate Alma tracking_id and error_code onto raised exceptions

tests.scope: Confirm that a real error response from Alma SANDBOX (GET on a synthetic invalid mms_id) carries a populated trackingId / errorCode through _handle_response onto the raised typed exception. Exercises the propagation path (#10 ACs: AlmaAPIError exposes tracking_id and alma_code; _handle_response populates them) end-to-end against live JSON.

needsHumanInput.description: A synthetic MMS ID known NOT to exist in SANDBOX. Used to deliberately trigger an error response so the parsed trackingId / errorCode can be observed on the raised exception. Do not use a real bib.

passCriteria: raised.tracking_id is a non-empty string (Alma populates trackingId on real error responses)
raised.alma_code is a non-empty string (Alma populates errorCode on real error responses)

unmappable.ac: Logger output formats include them (or domain code that already references them now actually receives values).
unmappable.reason: Verifies log-line content / downstream consumer behavior — not directly observable from a single API call. Covered by domain-code review (e.g., users.py already references getattr(e, 'tracking_id', None)).

unmappable.ac: Test fixture asserting both attributes round-trip from a representative error payload.
unmappable.reason: Pure-Python invariant — covered by the 10 tests in tests/unit/client/test_tracking_id_propagation.py with mocked HTTP; no Alma API surface to exercise beyond the smoke test t-10-1.
